summary: Fluoride flux protects soldered metals from oxidization but doesn't significantly remove previously formed oxide layers; instead, it helps maintain stable surface conditions crucial for reliable electrical connections over time. This supports option B indicating fluoride’s main effect is prevention through stabilization at an atomic level.
